symbols.name: SpinStrategy
symbols.detail: [USED] Transitively used by DefaultStrategy | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Abstract base class with no logic; nothing to evaluate. | [LEAN] Abstract base class for an explicitly documented extension point. ADR-003 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md) mandates subclassing over branching in engine.ts; the abstraction is the published API surface, not internal ceremony. | [NONE] Abstract base class with no test file found. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. Abstract base class with non-trivial extension semantics (strategy pattern, post-calculation hook) warrants at minimum a description and note about the abstract method contract.

symbols.name: DefaultStrategy
symbols.detail: [USED] Runtime-imported by 1 file: src/engine.ts | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Pass-through matches documented behavior exactly. | [LEAN] Pass-through implementation is justified by ADR-003: makes the no-adjustment case explicit instead of relying on a null-strategy guard. One importer confirms active use. | [NONE] No test file found. Used by src/engine.ts, so untested pass-through behavior in a critical path. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. Pass-through behavior is not obvious from the class name alone; a one-line doc clarifying it returns the result unchanged would suffice.

symbols.name: ConservativeStrategy
symbols.detail: [DEAD] Exported but imported by 0 files | [UNIQUE] No RAG data available | [OK] Math.floor(result.totalPayout * 0.8) matches the documented 0.8× floor factor in ADR-003 and both config guides. | [LEAN] 0 importers is a yellow flag, but ADR-003 lists it as a shipped concrete strategy alongside DefaultStrategy and documents its 0.8× RTP consequence. The docs treat both strategies as part of the library's public offering, not dead code. | [NONE] No test file found. The 0.8 multiplier with Math.floor truncation is business-critical and entirely untested. | [UNDOCUMENTED] No JSDoc comment. The 0.8× floor reduction is a non-obvious business rule with RTP implications that must be documented on the class itself, not inferred from reading the implementation.

actions.description: Add JSDoc documentation for exported symbol:  `SpinStrategy`, `DefaultStrategy`, `ConservativeStrategy`
Remove dead code: `ConservativeStrategy` is exported but unused

file_level.general_notes: Best practices score: 9.5/10 (1 FAIL, 2 WARN, 14 PASS)

rules.detail: No tsconfig provided, but the code emits no patterns that violate strict mode (no implicit any, no missing return types).
OOP strategy pattern is the documented design per ADR-003 (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md). No type assertions used.
File is too small to require utility types. `Readonly<SpinResult>` on parameters is covered under rule 5.

Both `adjustPayout` signatures accept `result: SpinResult` without expressing immutability intent. `Readonly<SpinResult>` as the parameter type would prevent accidental mutation and document the contract.
File uses only abstract class / class hierarchy and imports a single named type — no inconsistency.
No obvious lint violations. Consistent spacing, no unused bindings, abstract method correctly declared.

All three exported symbols — `SpinStrategy`, `DefaultStrategy`, `ConservativeStrategy` — lack JSDoc. The ADR and configuration docs describe their semantics; those descriptions should be surfaced at the declaration site.
Uses `import type`, spread syntax, abstract classes — no deprecated APIs.
Single type-only import from a sibling module; no side-effect imports, no circular dependencies apparent.
No asynchronous code. All methods are synchronous and return values directly.

Slot-machine/casino domain inferred from SpinResult, payout, RTP vocabulary and confirmed by .anatoly/docs/. `Math.floor(result.totalPayout * 0.8)` performs IEEE-754 floating-point multiplication on a monetary value. For bounded integer payouts this is practically safe, but regulated gaming requires deterministic precision — prefer `Math.floor(result.totalPayout * 4 / 5)` or integer arithmetic to avoid any FP edge case.
Single object spread with one arithmetic op — no performance concerns.
Both strategies are pure functions. `DefaultStrategy` is a pass-through; `ConservativeStrategy` is deterministic given integer input. Independently testable as noted in ADR-003.
No clear opportunity for `satisfies`, const type params, or `using` in this file. File is appropriately minimal.

Strategy pattern is the ADR-documented extension point for payout adjustment (.anatoly/docs/02-Architecture/04-Design-Decisions.md). `ConservativeStrategy` deliberately reduces RTP below 95% — this is documented, not a defect. No `Math.random()` in this file (RNG concerns belong in rng.ts).

suggestions.description: Add JSDoc to all three public exports to surface the ADR semantics at the declaration site.
Use `Readonly<SpinResult>` on the parameter to express immutability intent and prevent accidental mutation.
Replace float-factor multiplication with integer arithmetic for deterministic payout precision in regulated gaming context.
